codeReview.summary: Fail. The refactor cleanly splits `panel-settings.ts` and `panel-profiles.ts` into focused modules and keeps the touched files under 200 lines, but it does not preserve profile-editor persistence. Saving from the profile editor still ignores the API key, custom endpoint, and headers fields, so the feature does not fully satisfy `VAL-UI-002` / the expected provider-config persistence behavior.

issues.description: `collectProfileEditorData()` never reads `profileEditorApiKey`, `profileEditorEndpoint`, or `profileEditorHeaders`; instead it rebuilds `apiKey`, `customEndpoint`, and `extraHeaders` from `providerInstance` (`profile-editor.ts:91-112`). `saveProfileEdits()` then persists that helper output (`profile-editor.ts:150`), so edits made in the profile editor to provider credentials / endpoint / headers are discarded on save. Those fields are still part of the active UI contract (`ui/core/panel-elements.ts:122,126,128`) and are wired for input handling (`ui/core/event-handler-profile.ts:45-48`), so this breaks the expected 'Provider config saves correctly' / 'API keys persist' behavior for the settings feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `refactor-worker` skill says final validation must include `npm run test:integration` and that `followedProcedure` can be true only when all listed validation steps actually ran, but this worker skipped integration tests and still reported `followedProcedure: true`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/refactor-worker/SKILL.md:96-101,130 require `npm run test:integration`, `npm run lint`, and truthful `followedProcedure`; handoff `/Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/handoffs/2026-03-11T13-24-33-405Z__sidepanel-refactor-panel-settings__67843415-ab1a-4c66-9c77-52a94d5c1224.json`:14-36,58 records typecheck/build/lint/repo-standards/test:unit only and still sets `followedProcedure` to true.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission shared state says refactoring has a hard 200-line file limit, but the main repo-standards guardrail the workers rely on only reports `maxLines=300`, so the shared enforcement tool does not actually match the mission contract.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS `/Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/AGENTS.md`:9,13,39-40,182 require files to stay at or under 200 lines; this feature handoff reports `check:repo-standards` passing with `maxLines=300` (`.../handoffs/2026-03-11T13-24-33-405Z__sidepanel-refactor-panel-settings__67843415-ab1a-4c66-9c77-52a94d5c1224.json`:31-33), and the current settings area still contains `panel-provider-cards.ts` at 259 lines and `panel-oauth-providers.ts` at 254 lines.

summary: Reviewed the feature metadata, worker transcript skeleton, handoff, refactor-worker skill, mission shared-state files, and commit `a05e1a5`. The refactor structure itself is coherent, but the profile editor save path still drops edited provider credentials / endpoint / headers, so the feature does not fully meet `VAL-UI-002`; two shared-state observations were also recorded around skill compliance reporting and the mismatch between the mission's 200-line rule and repo-standards enforcement.
